screenplay_markdown.description: Full screenplay text covering every episode. Every scene must have: scene header (6 fields) + [OPENING STATE] + [MAIN BODY] in action blocks + [SCENE ENDING]. No episode may be replaced with a summary or placeholder.

scene_crosswalk.description: One entry per scene. Feeds directly into the storyboard scene_index. scene_id must match the SCENE heading in screenplay_markdown exactly.

scene_id.description: e.g. 01.01. Must match SCENE heading in screenplay_markdown.

time.description: Time of day for this scene.

slugline.description: e.g. INT. HALE CHARITY GALA - NIGHT

characters.description: Character names as they appear in the screenplay (display names).

core_event.description: One sentence: the central narrative event of this scene. Mirrors the scene header Core Event field.

scene_result.description: One sentence: how the scene ends / what changes. Mirrors the scene header Scene Result field.

hook_type.description: Episode-ending scenes: must use cliffhanger / reversal / emotional_peak / setup. Mid-episode scenes: none.

emotional_function.description: The emotional arc function of this scene within the episode.

character_ids.description: character_id values aligned with setting_bible and outline character_cards.

key_props.description: Optional. prop_id values for props that play a narrative role in this scene. Used by storyboard to pre-identify prop shots.
